title: Hooks, Configuration, Credentials, and Repository Trust · Repository Checkpoint
summary: A maintainer review compares near-miss operations for hooks, configuration, credentials, and repository trust and requires the smallest safe repository trust and local automation threat model with topology and content evidence. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for repository trust and local automation threat model. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Inspect configuration origin, scope, type, includes, conditional includes, worktree config, environment, and command overrides before trusting behavior.
Explain protected configuration and safe.directory ownership exceptions without using broad wildcards to conceal unsafe shared repositories.
Map client and server hook timing, working directory, environment, parameters, exit behavior, bypass paths, distribution, and enforcement limits.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# git-advanced cumulative repository evidence notebook
# Git operations change only disposable simulated repository state.
# Inspect status, diffs, graph, refs, and recovery before every mutation.

steps.title: Reconstruct the defensible process
steps.instruction: Order the Gita Config Origin Scope Include evidence cycle for this quiz task before implementing it.
steps.why: Correct order keeps Gita Config Origin Scope Include assumptions visible before execution and verifies its result against a boundary case afterward.

content.text: Case G87 is a quiz in a disposable repository. conditional user configuration injects a credential helper and hooks path for this repository. The learner must predict scope and origin identify the exact protected or local source of each effective value, run git config --show-origin --show-scope --get-regexp "^(core|credential|include|safe)\.", explain a global includeIf sets credential.helper while local core.hooksPath points into repository data, test this changed condition: change repository ownership and refuse a wildcard safe.directory exception, and preserve this recovery boundary: capture effective config, remove narrow trust grants, and rotate any exposed credential.

content.title: Ordering constraint
content.text: Order work so Gita Config Origin Scope Include has a declared contract before execution and a boundary or counterexample after it.

hints: Name the Gita Config Origin Scope Include precondition or invariant that the quiz case must preserve.

Trace this concrete case before choosing: Case G87 is a quiz in a disposable repository. conditional user configuration injects a credential helper and hooks path for this repository. The learner must predict scope and origin identify the exact protected or local source of each effective value, run git config --show-origin --show-scope --get-regexp "^(core|credential|include|safe)\.", explain a global includeIf sets credential.helper while local core.hooksPath points into repository data, test this changed condition: change repository ownership and refuse a wildcard safe.directory exception, and preserve this recovery boundary: capture effective config, remove narrow trust grants, and rotate any exposed credential.
Use the documented misconception as a counterexample target: git config --list reports values in decisive precedence order, so origin and scope are unnecessary.

options.text: Inspect repository boundary, config origin, current operation, worktree, index, refs, and graph
Preserve uncommitted work and a recovery ref, then state intended tree and topology
Preview and perform the least destructive simulated Git operation
Verify status, staged and range diffs, graph, tests, remote target, and recovery path

steps.title: Defend one difficult decision
steps.instruction: Write the quiz evidence note for Gita Untrusted Repository Defense: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — Case G94 is a quiz in a disposable repository. conditional user configuration injects a credential helper and hooks path for this repository. The learner must predict scope and origin identify the exact protected or local source of each effective value, run git config --show-origin --show-scope --get-regexp "^(core|credential|include|safe)\.", explain a global includeIf sets credential.helper while local core.hooksPath points into repository data, test this changed condition: change repository ownership and refuse a wildcard safe.directory exception, and preserve this recovery boundary: capture effective config, remove narrow trust grants, and rotate any exposed credential.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Inspect untrusted repositories as data in an isolated environment; disable execution paths, verify objects and config, and grant trust narrowly and reversibly.
